blocks.content: They didn't talk much on the ride home. Certain mornings needed time before they could become stories.
Eli broke first — laughing, couldn't stop. Savannah caught it from him. By the fourth block even Kit was gone, four kids losing it in the middle of an empty street.
She was smiling. Kit caught it twice and looked at the road both times.
They split at the corner — Eli east, Savannah south — and then it was just Kit and Daisy, riding slow on quiet streets. Someone's sprinkler caught them both and neither said anything about it.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I told you I wasn't a say-it person. Didn't say anything about a do-it person.
A pause. A full block of it.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Stick my foot through a rotten stair?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Make me stand outside a fence not knowing if you're coming.
He didn't have anything to say to that. The corner was right there.
